output.code:
# Let's read the file
path = "/mnt/data/file-1pi9B1ezjHFX67PrgMpanh-text_to_analyse.txt"
with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    text = f.read()
len(text), text[:5000]
print(text[-800:])

text = text
# Let's find indices of various markers
indices_quotes = [i for i, c in enumerate(text) if c == '"']
len(indices_quotes), indices_quotes[:20]

# Extract content between paired double quotes
pairs = list(zip(indices_quotes[::2], indices_quotes[1::2]))
segments = []
for start, end in pairs:
    segments.append((start, end, text[start+1:end]))
segments

# Find parentheses ranges
paren_open_indices = [i for i, c in enumerate(text) if c == '(']
paren_close_indices = [i for i, c in enumerate(text) if c == ')']
paren_open_indices[:10], paren_close_indices[:10], len(paren_open_indices), len(paren_close_indices)

# Helper to locate first span of a given substring and get its exact indices
def find_span(sub):
    start = text.find(sub)
    return start, start + len(sub)

# 1) First quoted ayah between indices 210-254 but we will recompute trimmed indices (though already trimmed inside quotes)
start1_content = pairs[0][0] + 1
end1_content = pairs[0][1]  # end index is index of the quote; exclusive end for content is this
span1 = (start1_content, end1_content, text[start1_content:end1_content])
span1

hadith1_str = "إن أحَدَكُمْ يُجْمَعُ خَلْقُهُ في بَطْنِ أُمِّهِ أَرْبَعِينَ يَوْمَاً نُطْفَةً، ثُمَّ يَكُونُ عَلَقَةً مِثْلَ ذلكَ، ثُمَّ يَكُونُ مُضْغَةً مِثْلَ ذلكَ، ثُمَّ يُرْسَلُ المَلَكُ فَيَنْفُخُ فِيهِ الرُّوحَ، وَيُؤْمَرُ بأرْبَعِ كَلِماتٍ: بِكَتْب رِزْقِهِ وَأجَلِهِ وَعَمَلِهِ وَشَقِيٍّ أَوْ سَعِيدٍ."
start_h1 = text.find(hadith1_str)
start_h1, hadith1_str[:50], start_h1!=-1

# Determine end index at just before the period '.' ending the hadith sentence
start_h1 = start_h1
end_h1_with_period = start_h1 + len(hadith1_str)
# The period is the last character of hadith1_str, confirm
hadith1_str[-1], text[end_h1_with_period-1:end_h1_with_period+1]

paraphrase_str = "وجعل فيها رواسي من فوقها وبارك فيها وقدر فيها أقواتها في أربعة أيام سواة للساكنين"
start_para = text.find(paraphrase_str)
start_para, text[start_para-50:start_para+len(paraphrase_str)+10]

start2 = pairs[1][0] + 1
end2 = pairs[1][1]
content2 = text[start2:end2]
content2[-5:], ord(content2[-1])

open_index = 1362
# We want substring starting after '(' and any leading whitespace; then end at before trailing punctuation and whitespace
start_h2_raw = open_index + 1
# Skip leading spaces
while start_h2_raw < len(text) and text[start_h2_raw].isspace():
    start_h2_raw += 1
# Now find newline index
newline_after = text.find("\n", start_h2_raw)
line_sub = text[start_h2_raw:newline_after]
line_sub[-20:], len(line_sub)

# Trim trailing punctuation and whitespace for Arabic punctuation "،", plus usual ., !, ?, :, ؛ etc.
def rstrip_punct(s):
    # Define punctuation to strip
    strip_chars = set(list(" ،؛:.,!؟…"))
    # plus ellipsis char '…' maybe used; yes included.
    # Remove trailing characters in strip_chars
    end = len(s)
    while end > 0 and (s[end-1] in strip_chars):
        end -= 1
    return s[:end], end

trimmed_line, trimmed_end = rstrip_punct(line_sub)
trimmed_line[-50:], trimmed_end, line_sub[trimmed_end:trimmed_end+10]

open_h3 = 2712
close_h3 = 2800
content_h3 = text[open_h3+1:close_h3]  # inside parentheses
content_h3, len(content_h3), content_h3[-5:]

r2a1 = "وَمَا مِنْ دَابَّةٍ فِي الْأَرْضِ إِلاَّ عَلَى اللَّهِ رِزْقُهَا"
start_r2a1 = text.find(r2a1)
start_r2a1, text[start_r2a1-20:start_r2a1+len(r2a1)+5]

r2a2 = "وَكَأَيِّنْ مِنْ دَابَّةٍ لا تَحْمِلُ رِزْقَهَا اللَّهُ يَرْزُقُهَا وَإِيَّاكُمْ"
start_r2a2 = text.find(r2a2)
start_r2a2, text[start_r2a2-10:start_r2a2+len(r2a2)+5]

r2a3 = "مَا يَفْتَحِ اللَّهُ لِلنَّاسِ مِنْ رَحْمَةٍ فَلا مُمْسِكَ لَهَا"
start_r2a3 = text.find(r2a3)
start_r2a3, text[start_r2a3-10:start_r2a3+len(r2a3)+5]

r2a4 = "وَإِنْ يُرِدْكَ بِخَيْرٍ فَلا رَادَّ لِفَضْلِهِ"
start_r2a4 = text.find(r2a4)
start_r2a4, text[start_r2a4-10:start_r2a4+len(r2a4)+5]

# Find the occurrence of the hadith "إنَّ أحَدَكُمْ يُجْمَعُ" from references [2]
occurrences = []
search_str = "إنَّ أحَدَكُمْ يُجْمَعُ خَلْقُهُ في بَطْنِ أُمِّهِ أَرْبَعِينَ يَوْمَاً نُطْفَةً، ثُمَّ يَكُونُ عَلَقَةً مِثْلَ ذلكَ، ثُمَّ يَكُونُ مُضْغَةً مِثْلَ ذلكَ، ثُمَّ يُرْسَلُ المَلَكُ فَيَنْفُخُ فِيهِ الرُّوحَ، وَيُؤْمَرُ بأرْبَعِ كَلِماتٍ: بِكَتْب رِزْقِهِ وَأجَلِهِ وَعَمَلِهِ وَشَقِيٍّ أَوْ سَعِيدٍ."
start_r2h = text.find(search_str)
start_r2h, text[start_r2h-20:start_r2h+30]

# Determine end index for r2 hadith: find period after the hadith string start; it's included in string we used (with period at end)
end_r2h = start_r2h + len(search_str) - 1  # exclude last '.' 
# Actually our search_str includes a trailing period; we want to exclude it
text[end_r2h-5:end_r2h+5]

# Find start of the first [3] ayah chunk "فِي يَوْمَيْنِ وَتَجْعَلُونَ ... لِّلسَّآئِلِينَ"
start_r3a1 = text.find("فِي يَوْمَيْنِ وَتَجْعَلُونَ")
# sometimes there might be tatweel or different quote; but we'll search "فِي يَوْمَيْنِ"
if start_r3a1 == -1:
    start_r3a1 = text.find("فِي يَوْمَيْنِ")
start_r3a1, text[start_r3a1-10:start_r3a1+50]

# find the closing decorative bracket after start_r3a1
close_marker_index = text.find("﴾", start_r3a1)
# The substring for ayah is from start_r3a1 to close_marker_index (exclusive)
start_r3a1, close_marker_index, text[close_marker_index-20:close_marker_index]

start_r3a2_marker = text.find("﴿", close_marker_index+1)
start_r3a2_content_start = start_r3a2_marker + 1
# find its closing '﴾'
close_r3a2 = text.find("﴾", start_r3a2_content_start)
start_r3a2_marker, start_r3a2_content_start, close_r3a2, text[start_r3a2_content_start:close_r3a2]

start_r3a3_marker = text.find("﴿", close_r3a2+1)
start_r3a3_content = start_r3a3_marker + 1
close_r3a3 = text.find("﴾", start_r3a3_content)
start_r3a3_marker, start_r3a3_content, close_r3a3, text[start_r3a3_content-5:start_r3a3_content+15]

snippet = text[4670:4905]
snippet

start_r3a4_marker = text.find("﴿", 4893+1)
start_r3a4_content = start_r3a4_marker + 1
# find the trailing ellipsis '...' and spaces. But to define the end, we might find '...' index and set end before that space preceding ellipsis.
three_dots_idx = text.find("...", start_r3a4_content)
# but maybe they use the three ascii dots rather than ellipsis char '…' earlier there is arabic bracket too. It's ' ... [...]' indeed
start_r3a4_marker, start_r3a4_content, three_dots_idx, text[start_r3a4_content: start_r3a4_content+60]

content_r3a4_full = text[5244:5426]
# It's ended before '...'
# rstrip spaces
content_r3a4_trim = content_r3a4_full.rstrip()
len_full = len(content_r3a4_full)
len_trim = len(content_r3a4_trim)
content_r3a4_full[-10:], len_full, len_trim, content_r3a4_trim[-10:]

spans = []

# 1) top quoted ayah
spans.append(("Aya", 211, 254, text[211:254]))

# 2) main hadith
start_h1 = 484
end_h1_excl = (start_h1 + len(hadith1_str)) - 1  # exclude the final dot
spans.append(("Hadeeth", start_h1, end_h1_excl, text[start_h1:end_h1_excl]))

# 3) paraphrase ayah: from "وجعل ...
start_para = 859
end_para = start_para + len(paraphrase_str)
spans.append(("Aya", start_para, end_para, text[start_para:end_para]))

# 4) quoted ayah in double quotes from surah Nahl (with trailing space trimmed)
start2 = 986 + 1
end2 = 1134
# trim trailing whitespace
while text[end2 - 1].isspace():
    end2 -= 1
spans.append(("Aya", start2, end2, text[start2:end2]))

# 5) hadith inside unclosed parentheses: compute start minimal and end trimmed of trailing punctuation
start_h2 = start_h2_raw  # previously computed var; but we need to re-evaluate from text again if not in scope
start_h2_raw

# recompute the second hadith substring indices robustly
open_index = paren_open_indices[0]  # 1362
start_h2 = open_index + 1
# skip leading whitespace
while start_h2 < len(text) and text[start_h2].isspace():
    start_h2 += 1
newline_after = text.find("\n", start_h2)
line_sub = text[start_h2:newline_after]
def rstrip_punct_endpos(s):
    strip_chars = set(list(" ،؛:.,!؟…"))
    end = len(s)
    while end > 0 and (s[end-1] in strip_chars):
        end -= 1
    return end
endpos_in_line = rstrip_punct_endpos(line_sub)
end_h2 = start_h2 + endpos_in_line
# store
spans.append(("Hadeeth", start_h2, end_h2, text[start_h2:end_h2]))
(start_h2, end_h2, text[start_h2:end_h2][-30:])

# hadith in references [1]
start_h3 = paren_open_indices[1] + 1  # 2712+1=2713
end_h3 = paren_close_indices[0]       # 2800
# trim leading space
while text[start_h3].isspace():
    start_h3 += 1
# trim trailing spaces and punctuation before closing paren? There might be final spaces; not in this case.
end_h3_trim = end_h3
while text[end_h3_trim-1].isspace() or text[end_h3_trim-1] in " ،؛:.,!؟…":
    end_h3_trim -= 1
spans.append(("Hadeeth", start_h3, end_h3_trim, text[start_h3:end_h3_trim]))
(start_h3, end_h3_trim, text[start_h3:end_h3_trim])

spans.append(("Aya", start_r2a1, start_r2a1 + len(r2a1), text[start_r2a1:start_r2a1+len(r2a1)]))
spans.append(("Aya", start_r2a2, start_r2a2 + len(r2a2), text[start_r2a2:start_r2a2+len(r2a2)]))
spans.append(("Aya", start_r2a3, start_r2a3 + len(r2a3), text[start_r2a3:start_r2a3+len(r2a3)]))
spans.append(("Aya", start_r2a4, start_r2a4 + len(r2a4), text[start_r2a4:start_r2a4+len(r2a4)]))
spans[-4:]

# hadith in references [2]
start_r2h = text.find(search_str)
end_r2h = start_r2h + len(search_str) - 1  # exclude period
spans.append(("Hadeeth", start_r2h, end_r2h, text[start_r2h:end_r2h]))
(start_r2h, end_r2h, text[start_r2h:end_r2h][-30:])

spans.append(("Aya", start_r3a1, close_marker_index, text[start_r3a1:close_marker_index]))
spans[-1]

spans.append(("Aya", start_r3a2_content_start, close_r3a2, text[start_r3a2_content_start:close_r3a2]))
spans[-1]

spans.append(("Aya", start_r3a3_content, close_r3a3, text[start_r3a3_content:close_r3a3]))
spans[-1]

spans.append(("Aya", start_r3a4_content, start_r3a4_content + len(content_r3a4_trim), text[start_r3a4_content:start_r3a4_content + len(content_r3a4_trim)]))
spans[-1], text[start_r3a4_content + len(content_r3a4_trim):start_r3a4_content + len(content_r3a4_trim)+10]

# Build citations list verifying extraction coherence
citations = []
for typ, start, end, substr in spans:
    # verify substring matches text
    assert text[start:end] == substr, (typ, start, end, substr[:30])
    citations.append({
        "span_start": start,
        "span_end": end,
        "span_text": substr,
        "citation_type": "Aya" if typ=="Aya" else "Hadeeth"
    })
len(citations), citations[0], citations[-1]

for i, c in enumerate(citations):
    print(i+1, c["citation_type"], c["span_start"], c["span_end"], "|", c["span_text"][:80], "...")
